draft.introduction: The Vishnu Purana opens with Parasara declaring the nature of Vishnu as the supreme being from whom the universe arises, in whom it subsists, and into whom it dissolves.

translation.text: Parasara said: I will relate to you the creation of the world. Vishnu is the cause of creation, preservation, and destruction. He is the root of the world. The world consists of Vishnu.
translation.original_text: parāśara uvāca | viṣṇor utsṛṣṭaṃ sthitiṃ kṣobhaṃ ca kāraṇam

translation.text: He is Brahma the creator, Vishnu the preserver, and Rudra the destroyer. He himself creates, himself preserves, himself destroys. The three forms are but three functions of one being.
translation.original_text: brahmā srashta tathā viṣṇuḥ sthiteḥ | rudro yaḥ saṃharate

translator_notes.note: The Vishnu Purana’s theology of trimūrti—three forms of one God—parallels the Christian Trinity in structure while differing in content. Brahma creates, Vishnu sustains, Shiva destroys, but all three are functions of a single divine reality.
